products.title: MARCELO ANAEROBIC 1606
products.body_html:
Farmer: Marcelo Assis & Flavio Silva

Origin: Campos Altos, Brazil

Process: Anaerobic Natural

Varietal: Paraïso

Roast level: light

In the cup: orange wine, lemon verbena, plum

Marcelo's coffee is nothing new on our menu. This lot, however, is. This is a sort of comp lot he has offered us. The process for this coffee is as follows...

Anaerobic fermentation in stainless steel tankes, temp controlled to not
exceed 28o C. Dried in thick layers on cement patios for 2 days before being spread into thinner layers for 5 - 8 more days.

This has given the coffee an incredible mouthfeel and sweetness beyond the normal Marcelo offering. Tons of cherryluxardo cherry, orange wine, & soft florals make this a VERY easy to drink Brazilian offering that has more than just the peanut butter/chocolate bomb we're accustomed to. Enjoy it early & often!

products.title: SUPER SUCRÉ 2K26
products.body_html:
Farmer: David Sabillon

Origin: Las Flores, Honduras

Process: honey

Varietal: Paraneima

Roast level: medium

In the cup: maple taffy, milk chocolate, persimmon

products.title: SPRING BREAKERS 2K26
products.body_html:
Farmer: Cesario "Gio" Fernandez

Origin: Las Flores, Honduras

Process: natural

Varietal: Paraneima

Roast level: light

In the cup: marmalade, pomegranate, Payday bar

products.title: LEKALI ESTATE - ESPRESSO ROAST
products.body_html:
Farmer: Nima Tenzing

Origin: Nukawot, Nepal

Process: Mosto washed dry fermentation

Varietal: Bourbon & Caturra

Roast level: Superlight

In the cup: mulberry, cranberry, Oolong tea

Lekali Estate — Nima Tenzing
Produced by Nima Tenzing at Lekali Estate in Nepal, this VERY RARE Himalayan coffee is BIG funk, crisp apple like tartness & juicy berry notes…it’s something that, if you can afford it, you shouldn’t miss.

products.title: sɨmᵾłȺŧɨøn PEPE JIJON SY-OXY 🏔️ 100g
products.body_html:
Farmer: Pepe Jijon

Origin: Ecuador

Process: Anoxic & anaerobic washed

Varietal: Sidra

Roast level: Superlight

In the cup: mango, matcha, summiting

products.title: sɨmᵾłȺŧɨøn MORKIE 75 🪻 100g
products.body_html:
Farmer: Bura, Bensa, Sidama

Origin: Ethiopia

Process: 72h hand pulped white honey

Varietal: 74158 Heirloom

Roast level: Superlight

In the cup: mango candy, purple flowers, outer space

products.title: ＰＯＳＡ ＤＥ ＣＨＲＩＳＴＯ (STEEZUS)
products.body_html:
Farmer: Edin Ever Sabillon, Nery Martinez,

Origin: Las Flores, Santa Barbara, Honduras

Process: washed & natural

Varietal: Paraneima

Roast level: Light

In the cup: cacao nibs, lime zest, sangria

This spring, rise to the occasion & brew a coffee of nostalgic 32bit biblical proportions. Posa De Christo is made up of 2 different Paraneima lots, one washed & one natural from two producers; Nery & Edin. This coffee is full of that wild Paraneima flavour profile: The washed coffees often taste bright, tropical, & have this REALLY interesting tea-like note finish almost like sage/thyme that we find so amazing while the naturals are heavy dark ripe fruits, cacao nibs, & massive body. When these two coffees are blended together, it creates the the best of both worlds...this one & the next ; )

«Il est parmis nous !...STEEZUS» 🌤🧔🏽🛹

PARANEIMA

Parainema is a coffee varietal developed in Honduras by IHCAFE as part of a national effort to combat coffee leaf rust while improving cup quality.

It was officially released in the early 2000s (around 2002–2004) after years of field trials and selection.

Genetically, it comes from Timor Hybrid lineage (for rust resistance) crossed with traditional Arabica types—meaning it was built for resilience first, but with flavour in mind.

Where it grows

Primarily in Honduras, especially:

  • Santa Bárbara
  • El Paraíso

Best expression tends to show at:

  • 1200–1700 MASL
  • Cooler, slower-growing conditions → more complex sugars

products.title: LES FRUITS DU "BLOOM" 🍃
products.body_html:
Farmer: Sebastian Ramirez

Origin: El Placer, Colombia

Process: Anaerobic natural

Varietal: Pink Bourbon

Roast Level: Superlight

In the cup: chocolate liqueur, purple flowers, grape jelly

Sebastien Ramirez is no stranger to us & not a new addition to our menu. This coffee in particular though, is ver much a new addition. Called "Midnight Bloom" by Seb, we thought it to be an obvious fit for our Fruits Du Bloom sku.

This coffee was produced using anaerobic fermentation on MOSTLY ripe cherries (5% semi ripe) & a LONG fermentation time of 200HOURS! We won't get into the nitty gritty but properly fermenting cherries for 200hrs without incurring mold & other negative impacting bacteria is SO HARD & requires meticulous monitoring.

Sebastian Ramirez is a pioneering Colombian coffee producer known for pushing the boundaries of fermentation and flavor design. Working at the forefront of experimental processing, he combines high-Brix cherry selection with extended anaerobic and co-fermentation techniques to create intensely aromatic, fruit-driven coffees that challenge traditional definitions of terroir. His work has helped redefine what modern specialty coffee can taste like, earning recognition among top roasters and competitors worldwide.

products.title: BIG TREE
products.body_html:
Farmer: Big Tree Estate family farm

Origin: Kitale, Kenya

Process: Anaerobic Natural

Varietal: SL28 & Batian

Roast Level: Superlight

In the cup: cherry soda, chocolate bar, apricot

products.title: LEKALI ESTATE
products.body_html:
Farmer: Nima Tenzing

Origin: Nukawot, Nepal

Process: Mosto washed dry fermentation

Varietal: Bourbon & Caturra

Roast level: Superlight

In the cup: mulberry, cranberry, Oolong tea

Lekali Estate — Nima Tenzing
Produced by Nima Tenzing at Lekali Estate in Nepal, this VERY RARE Himalayan coffee is BIG funk, crisp apple like tartness & juicy berry notes…it’s something that, if you can afford it, you shouldn’t miss.

products.title: Odd Coffee - Collaboration avec Messorem
products.body_html:
Farmer: Wilton Benitez & Eduardo Pereira

Origin: Brazil & Colombia

Process: Washed & Natural

Varietals: Blend

Roast level: Dark

In the cup: chocolate cake, almonds, cherries

products.title: BEKELE YUTUTE COE "AFRO FUNK #8"
products.body_html:
Farmer: Bekele Yutute

Origin: Sidamo, Ethiopia

Process: Natural anaerobic

Varietal: 74158 Heirloom

Roast level: Light

In the cup: boysenberry, honeydew melon, white strawberry

In 2024, Bekele Yutute placed 4th at Cup Of Excellence. This is a reproduction of that lot. An anaerobic natural with tons of incredible complexity. It needs lots of time to rest. It needs care when brewing, but it's such a rewarding, delicious coffee. Super sweet, floral, a tad funky...this coffee has everything in it. It's one of the best lots we had from that harvest. Please enjoy and drink often ; )

ABOUT BEKELE

Bekele Yutute farms just under 4 hectares in the highlands of Bensa, Sidama, at elevations reaching 2,300–2,350 meters. The farm, Denbi, grows primarily 74158, a JARC selection known for cup quality and resistance to coffee berry disease.

For many years Bekele sold cherries to cooperatives. In recent seasons he began processing and exporting his own coffee, working with small family-run drying sites including Kokose and Murago.

His coffees are typically natural or anaerobic natural, dried slowly on raised beds after meticulous cherry selection.

The lot we purchased is part of a wave of coffees from the region that are redefining what Ethiopian naturals can be.

products.title: NATURALINK ꩜
products.body_html:
Farmer: Lenin Flores

Origin: El Morito, Peru

Process: Washed

Varietal: yellow Caturra

Roast level: Light

In the cup: marmalade, almond roca, pancakes

products.title: ROBERTA & EDUARDO
products.body_html:
Farmer: Roberta & Eduardo Soares Pereira

Origin: Cerrado Mineiro, Brazil

Process: anaerobic yeast fermentation

Varietal: Acaua Novo

Roast level: Light

In the cup: chocolate ganache, violets, lulo

products.title: SOLARIS ☀️ Peru Natural
products.body_html:
Farmer: Salomon Estela

Origin: El Pino, Peru

Process: Natural

Varietal: Marshell

Roast level: Light

In the cup: 7 UP, malt chocolate, cranberry

ABOUT SALOMON

Farming at 1850–1870 masl, Salomon Estela is a second-generation coffee grower—and father to Miguel Estela—known for his hospitality, experience, and standout Marshell Naturals. Salomon’s farm sees more visitors than any other in the network, a testament to his welcoming presence and deep agronomic knowledge. His motivation is family, and his ambition is to see his coffee recognized in every market it reaches.

products.title: Marina Orellana
products.body_html:
Farmer: Marina Orellana

Origin: Las Flores, Honduras

Process: Washed

Varietal: Pacas

Roast level: Light

In the cup: yellow plum, caramel popcorn, nougat - silky sweetness with a round, comforting finish.

New producer alert! This is a nano lot from Marina and our first year working with her.

Marina farms coffee in Las Flores, Santa Bárbara, one of Honduras’ most celebrated coffee regions near Lake Yojoa. High elevations and volcanic soils produce sweet, balanced coffees with classic Central American structure

products.title: Yellow Diamond
products.body_html:
Farmer: Habil Olakes

Origin: West Rift Valley, Kenya

Process: Anaerobic Natural

Varietal: Batian & Ruiru 11

Roast level: Light

In the cup: plums, apple cider, honey cake - deep fruit, warm spice, and a syrupy finish

Habil Olakes moved his family out to Western Kenya to get away from the city, and back to his roots. They started farming corn and chickens, flowers and honey. They kept the latter two, but turned their focus to producing coffee after hearing the enthusiasm of a friend and neighbor of theirs—Stephen Nendela of Muinami Estate who you might recognize from Juice Bomb and other bags ; )

products.title: 𝐓𝐄𝐂𝐇𝐍𝐎𝐍𝐀𝐓𝐔𝐑𝐄
products.body_html:
Farmer: Dominga Carrasco

Origin: El Amenecer, Peru

Process: Washed

Varietal: yellow Caturra

Roast level: Light

In the cup: cherry soda, chocolate bar, apricot

products.title: ‧₊˚❀༉‧₊˚. Bouquet. 𝒷𝓁𝑜𝓈𝓈𝑜𝓂𝑒𝒹 𝑒𝒹𝒾𝓉𝒾𝑜𝓃
products.body_html:
Farmer: Jairo Arcila, Eliyas Dukamo & Atiklit Dejene

Origin: Colombia & Ethiopia

Process: honey & co-ferment

Altitude: 2000masl

Varietal: 74110 and Pink Bourbon

Roast level: light

In the cup: rose petals, white chocolate, love letters

This is a new blend from the cupping lab here at Traffic. We have some beautiful Ethiopians blended with a very novel, very FLORAL, Rose petal co-fermented pink bourbon! The result is the most floral cup of coffee you have ever had!

products.title: «Collab» avec Béton Armé 🔥🤘🔥 100g
products.body_html:
Whoah WHOAH WHOAH!!! ✊

Voici notre nouvelle « collab » avec la scène musicale émergente Montréalaise : BÉTON ARMÉ X TRAFFIC

https://open.spotify.com/intl-fr/artist/7B9AYoMBJAGlHtB97cPt9z?si=7kAvRFekTeKQMyEeRiPo8w

Comme vous savez, notre team est un belle gang de fervants de musique, de « guit » électrique , de ballade dans les mosh pit et on aime notre ville: Montréal notre cité 🖤

Dans le cadre de leur prochaine tournée aux USA 🇺🇸, Béton Armé, ce band de malade à la réputation incroyable et internationale (ça n’est presque plus émergent à ce point ci 🤔…) On a crée avec eux et leur artiste fétiche @beanmash une super boite de café qui vous allez pouvoir retrouver à leur show pendant leur tournée aux USA 🇺🇸 (btw here’s a shortcut to get our coffee fellow american,, they’ll have our stuff all along their tour, Don’t miss out!!! 👀)

Merci Béton 🖤 vous êtes vrais, votre musique tue avec tendresse. Bonne tournée!

l’union fait la force 🤝

Credit à JESSE RAMIREZ pour la photo de groupe

Voici le café

Farmer: Wilton Benitez

Origin: La Macarena, Cauca, Colombia

Altitude: 1800m

Process: Washed

Varietal: Castillo

Notes: tangerine, amande grillés, chocolat

Roast: Light

products.title: Édition limité de la ST-JEAN-Guy ⚜️⚜️⚜️
products.body_html:
Farmer: Wilton Benitez & Eduardo Pereira

Farm: Granja Paraiso 92 & Bioma Cafe

Origin: Colombia, Brazil

Process: Natural

Altitude: ~1750-2050m

Varietal: 74110

Roast level: Medium-dark

In the cup: Luxardo, dark chocolate cake, coffee on the patio

Jean Guy.

Growing up this name was such a big part of our lives here in Quebec. Maybe your bus driver had that name, or the guy that worked at the depanneur near your house, or maybe it was a neighbour or relative. To us it’s kind of that name that is synonymous with the every day “habitant” Québécois. This coffee was born of memories, "des souvenirs" of so many different things…the beginnings of Quebec & Montréal, our very distinct & celebrated seasons, and the traditions that come along with them. This is not a light roast coffee because most people in Quebec are not light roast people. They’re old school. What this coffee does differently is that there is an extra layer…a “ché PAS, maaan” of jammy floral fruit that represents the wonderful fruit harvests that we are privy to in this province. Think wild berries from Saguenay & île d’orléans & baies d’argousiers from Kamouraska. That extra layer also represents the uniqueness that makes us different here in “La Belle Province.” So it is a dark roast, but it’s not a dark roast that you’ve ever had before.

The packaging was inspired by the road trips that we all have taken throughout "La Belle Province", the flora and Fauna that we are able to witness come, go, and evolve throughout the years, and the customs that we all continue to pass on today.

You could say in some ways this coffee isn’t for you but instead for us. A little way of tooting our own horn over here in the Europe of Canada. You can always join in with us though because there’s always a log that you can pull up and sit down at this bonfire.

products.title: PIE FACE V. 2 🥧
products.body_html:
Farmer: Jairo Arcila

Origin: Colombia

Process: Various co-ferments

Varietal: Castillo, Pink Bourbon, Caturra

Roast level: Medium-light

In the cup: cinnamon brioche, strawberry jam, chocolate tart

Pie Face is an experiment. It's a crazy explosion of flavours from combining some of our exceptional co-ferments. This blend is NOT for the faint of heart or anyone still upset about co-ferments existing. We think this is a cool coffee to stash in your freezer for an afternoon delight, & make your favourite coffee mocktails with & it continues to support the people we work with in other coffees that are more accessible and part of their less labour intensive harvests.

products.title: Half-Caf PBJ
products.body_html:
Farmers: Marcelo Assis & Flavio Silva

Origin: Finca Ohlos D'agua, Cerrado Mineiro, Brazil

Altitude: ~1206-1800m

Process: pulped natural/ethyl acetate/co-fermentation

Varietal: Castillo, Paraiso, Colombia

Roast level: light/medium

Notes: banana, PB & jam

Ever want to drink a sandwich? Me neither! But wow! What a cool coffee this is.

Made with a honey style processed BANANA co-ferment, this half-caf is an incredible afternoon delight. Try it over ice with coconut milk and a nice coconut cream whip if you dare!

products.title: ⋆｡°✩ Booty Call 2026 ✩°｡⋆
products.body_html:
Origin: Colombia & Honduras

Altitude: ~1730m-2250m

Process: Honey co-ferment & washed

Varietal: Pink bourbon, Paraneima

Roast Level: Light-medium

In the cup: peach, coconut whipped cream, booty calls

Here's a wild one for you & your valentine to brew together or you can brew it solo and give yourself all the love...it's your love to give or share as you choose. This blend is made up of a wonderful coffee from Santa Barbara, Honduras and Jairo Arcila's Honey processed peach co-ferment. It's a ridiculously juicy, THICCC cup that hits in all the right spots. Don't be too scared of co-ferments in this context. On their own they can be overwhelming, but in a blend like this, we think you're up to it. Do you accept the challenge? Hope so, we're aiming to be your fave booty call ; )

products.title: Vintërfika
products.body_html:
Origin: Costa Rica & Colombia

Process: Anaerobic natural & co-ferment

Altitude: 1525-2000 masl

Varietal: Castillo and Sarchimor

Roast level: Medium

In the cup: Swedish Cardamom buns, gingerbread cookie, milk chocolate

Aptly named "Vintërfika", this is THE perfect coffee for those cozy winter mornings at home, a brisk walk around the lake, or your "Après Ski" traditions at your favourite mountain! What can we say, we're canucks and we love winter! This blend is made with some really nice coffees, including one of Jairo's cardamom co-ferments that we've been waiting to receive. This coffee is NOT a super light roast, but it's still VERY fragrant, sweet, & hits on a winters day like nothing else. 

Get your skis shined up and grab a stick of juicy fruit....or a bag of this Vintërfika, cuz it's gonna get gone real fast.

products.title: BASHA BEKELE DUWANCHO
products.body_html:
Farmer: Basha Bekele & ~100 small farmers

Origin: Bensa, Ethiopia

Process: Natural

Varietal: 74158 Heirloom

In the cup: blood orange, Aperol, pear, long sunsets

From the same place and people that brought us Nguisse Nare comes BOMBE DUWANCHO. High up on a ridge at 2,300 MASL in Bombe, Duwancho sits among the highest-altitude washing stations in all of Ethiopia. We’ve had our eyes (and cups) on this region for years, and it keeps delivering — consistently placing among the top performers in the Ethiopian Cup of Excellence. The elevation here pushes the coffee to develop exceptionally dense seeds, giving way to those vibrant, layered profiles we chase every season.

Producer Basha took home first place at the COE unsurprisingly but, we all knew it was inevitable. These coffees have become some of our all-time favourites — and clearly, we’re not the only ones.

products.title: Santa Margarita
products.body_html:
Farmer: Santa Margarita / Camila Topke

Origin: Chimaltenango, Acatenango

Altitude: 1640m

Process: washed, dried on patios and on raised beds

Varietal: Caturra, Sarchimor, Catimor, Geisha

Roast level: DARK

Notes: molasses, dark chocolate, walnuts, buttery

This super cool single origin is roasted real old school so, at home, it's a breeze to pull an espresso. This iteration of this coffee reminded us of our grandmothers, who back in the day, would eat molasses on bread as a dessert! Times were real tough back then in La Belle Province but "Grandm'man" made it through cuz they made em tough back in the day! Super delicious, produced uniquely by girl power in Guatemala, we hope to continue to have the privilege of buying from them and roasting up this great green as a 90's style throwback to old school coffee.

Finca Santa Margarita is the family farm of Camila Topke, a trader at InterAmerican Coffee. Santa Margarita is in large part operated by women. In addition to women coffee pickers, women are in charge of the grafting program and of running the farm's school and health clinic.

Camila is a member of the International Women's Coffee Alliance (IWCA) and for a time was vice president of the IWCA Guatemala chapter. She was also instrumental in developing Santa Margarita's forward-looking environmental programs and is still very active in its management.

Finca Santa Margarita

The farm was founded in 1838 by Thomas Wyld (Camila’s great grandfather) and has been in the family or four generations. Today, Santa Margarita is 2,320 hectares—812 of which are coffee farm—with Caturra, Sarchimor, Catimor and Geisha varieties. The rest is dedicated natural forest land, devoted to the promotion of natural species and wildlife biodiversity.

The farm has three springs, which are protected by environmental policies and provide water to the people living on the farm, as well as to the wet mill.

Environmental sustainability is a key mission of Finca Santa Margarita, from the moment seeds are planted to the moment coffee leaves the farm. Its management practices are based on an agroforestry system that includes low intakes of agrochemicals, the use of organic, compost-based soil nutrition, high shade coverage and sustainable quality standards.

The farm also takes very seriously the quality of life of the workers on the farm. It houses an elementary school for children who live on Santa Margarita, as well as neighboring farms (there are 75 children in the school in total). The farm also includes a small church and health clinic that partner with the Guatemala Dept. of Health.

Processing

Coffee is washed and dried on patios, as well as on raised African beds. A goal of the farm is to build a cupping facility on site and to train workers to cup and be advocates for quality.

products.title: GOOD VIBRATIONS 🎵🎶
products.body_html:
Origin: Colombia & Ethiopia

Process: Washed co-ferment & washed

Altitude: ~1730-2250m

Varietal: purple Caturra & 74111 Heirloom

Roast level: Medium

In the cup: watermelon, jasmine tea, "rosé" all day

Spend winter on an island in the sun!

Maybe don't mix any of this with your Whiteclaw but, you can still make insane cold brew to bring to the beach or brew some up and chuck it in a tumbler for later. This blend is meant to bring 2 complimentary coffees together for a super unique experience. You see, with these crazy co-ferments, there are many times people don't want to have a full "melt your face off" (or "melt your wallet") cup of coffee but you still want to enjoy some insanely juicy/funky/special brew...what are the youth to do? Well, they can try one of these! This is the 1st of a whole array of blends we're coming in hot with that will feature our fave experimental coffees of the season.

Please do enjoy the "Good Vibrations" of this "Assemblage de Plage" any way you choose. It's a good thing.

products.title: Marcelo Assis Nogueira
products.body_html:
Farmer: Marcelo Assis Nogueira

Farm: Ohlos D'agua

Origin: Campos Altos, Brazil

Process: Natural

Varietal: Paraïso & Arara

In the cup: Reese's, pomegranate, thyme & apricot

This marks the 4th year we've bought the most exceptional coffee offered by Marcelo. Marcelo & Flavio's commitment to quality & excellence is why, for years to come, we will be working with them & Bioma/Aequitas, on these farms. This year, this natural is so clean, you'd think it was a washed coffee. Sweet, rich, very heavy body that pairs beautifully with milk, & as an espresso, finishes with a very subtle lime/tropical note. VERY low acidity coffee for those who aren't fond of bright cups ; )

About the Farm

Bioma Café’s story begins back in 2001 when Marcelo Nogueira Assis and Flavio Marcio Silva met each other and committed their life purposes in farming. Both, Marcelo and Flavio, were born in families that carry the agriculture DNA. Flavio who has vast experience in managing the family businesses met Marcelo who was a recent graduate in the technical school of agriculture and cattle and was eager to explore the farming universe. As a result, they formed a partnership based on love for agriculture, respect, and reciprocity.

Coffee is a passion for most people that live in Cerrado, in the state of Minas Gerais and it wasn't different for the young entrepreneurs who were born and raised among coffee plantations. In 2010, an opportunity arose and they constituted the first farm, focused on specialty coffee production in Campos Altos region, Minas Gerais. They carefully evaluated each piece of land that would receive the coffee crops, after all the purpose was of producing extraordinary coffees. After a long period looking for the perfect terroir, the farm location was selected, a place that produces the most incredible coffees in the high plateaus of Cerrado Mineiro. The Cerrado is one of the richest biomes in the world in terms of biodiversity, and Bioma Café emerged with the purpose of producing specialty coffees maintaining the balance between agriculture and the Cerrado biome.

products.title: Minute Papillon DECAF
products.body_html:
Farmer: Grupo San Augustin Los Cauchos

Origin: Cauca, Colombia

Process: washed, oxygen sugarcane decaf

Altitude: 1800-2000m

Varietal: Caturra & Typica

Roast level: Medium

In the cup: cookie dough, blood orange, afternoon yoga

This is NOT your regular decaf. None of the decaf coffees we source are. This one, however, holds a special place in our hearts. Years ago, when we still were trying to understand why decaf sucked so much, we found a few examples of really fantastically processed decafs but, we realized there had to be a few boxes ticked off every time.

One - It had to be a great coffee to start with, not a coffee that didn't turn out & the only thing left was to make it a decaf.

Two - It had to be decaffeinated at origin. Not only from a freshness perspective but also to reduce as much of the carbon footprint as possible. Coffee is already a product that travels across the world and sometimes back again, so any small element we can improve is a win.

Three - It had to be the best decaf we ever had. While this decaf remains the highest we've paid ever for a decaf, it IS by far the best decaf we've ever drank so, you get what you pay for.

The San Augustin & El Alto de Los Idolos

Around 1.000 B.C. a pre-Colombian civilization known as San Agustín ﬂourished next to the mountains where the Magdalena River comes to life. The civilization disappeared mysteriously before the arrival of the Spanish Conquerors. Little is known as to exactly what happened and the only thing that was left behind were the burial sites. These sites were discovered in 1960 by explorers and the archaeological park “El Alto de Los Idolos”, the home to these burial sites, was founded.

The coffee growers from this region initially got together to share ideas and form groups to facilitate the sale of their coffees. The group has always been quality focused and members currently working on standardizing practices, improving their overall processes as well as their drying patios. They have managed to signiﬁcantly increase the quality of their coffee over time.

products.title: Olympus Espresso
products.body_html:
Origin: South America

Process: Natural/washed

Roast level: Medium-dark

In the cup: Reese's chocolate, salted butter caramel, the early 2000s

The “Olympus” is that constant workhorse, ready to perform every day. It's an old-school vibe of big creamy peanut butter, chocolate, & toffee with a small bit of candied orange that accents the syrupy sweet notes. Cuts through milk like gangbusters so you're never drinking...well, just a cup of warm milk :/

This blend is seasonal, as are all of our blends but we try to always keep the origins the same. Fresh Brazil from our favourite micro-lot projects and some super sweet Central & South American coffees that have won our cupping tables each quarter. Is this coffee super funky? NO. It's not supposed to be.

It's about the early 2000's at the beginning of specialty coffee in Montreal. It's your favourite pair of skate shoes. Your favourite Montreal Haunt. Your best friends.

This coffee is about the tried and true, the "always there for you".

products.title: Dante Street Espresso
products.body_html:
Origin: Central & South America + Africa

Process: Natural & washed

Roast level: Super dark

In the cup: dark chocolate, tobacco flower & liquorice

This sweet, rich, syrupy espresso is exactly what Nono & Nona will approve of. They’re harsh critics, & rightly so, it’s a tradition to uphold. While our interpretation of Italian roast contains no Robusta, this coffee is pushed to the deep, dark reaches of coffee roasting to give you that bold taste that pairs outstandingly with milk, or as an espresso with your favourite zeppoli!

The Story behind "Dante Street"

Dante St. ….what can I say...I smell pizza, I smell cannolis, coffee....I feel like an Italian in my city. When I was a child my best friend's parents brought us to Quincallerie Dante (ON DANTE) it was such a polarizing shop, I thought. Guns & Kitchen gear. I was too young to make the distinction between hunting weapons & anything you’d see on COPS. Still, the store was magical to me & remains one of the places I visit every time I’m in Little Italy. That area is full of life, spirit, & that same unidentifiable quality I found so charming all those years ago. You could say this is my tip of the hat to the Italians I grew up with, & the ones that still put up with me today!

products.title: Milkshake Espresso
products.body_html:
Origin: Kenya & Ethiopia

Process: washed & natural

Altitude: ~1700-2200m

Varietal: various JARC Landraces & combination of SL's and ruiru 11, Batian

Roast level: Medium

Notes: upside down pineapple cake, raspberry, peach

Originally an ode to one of our favourite espresso blends from the UK, the milkshake morphed into our tribute to a director that we love...David Lynch.

It's a dessert cart at your favourite old school diner, it's memories of your grandmothers angel food cake with cut-up strawberries and fresh cream in summer, it's nostalgic movies that make us feel good. This is easily one of the best blends we've made. It changes every year because the crops rotate but we will always aim to keep it fruit forward but round, juicy but not too bright.

Made with coffees we'd serve as Single Origins, as usual, this blend is of the utmost quality. It's something we do differently here at Traffic...because we care ; )

Prices may rise & fall throughout the year based on what kind of prices we receive as a final price for the coffees we're roasting for this blend. WHEN we can reduce the prices we will but, keep in mind, these are coffees from inherently expensive origins and we're sourcing from our long time partners so, they have final say in what their coffees are worth. We hope you agree : )

PULLING THE MILKSHAKE

We recommend larger shots, so, a larger ratio of dry to wet.

EXAMPLE: 17g ground coffee = 42.5-45g of espresso (in 29-31 seconds)

(This is a 1:2.5-2.75 ratio)

IF you age this 2 months or + you can sometimes compress that ratio to close to a 1:2-1:2.25 ratio which is concentrated but PACKED with flavour! Both ways work, play with it!

ALSO, don't be afraid of making this coffee as a pour over or a batch of drip. It's awesome like that too!
